raw_consumer_statement:
Ive tried to cancel my XXXX subscription for around 4 years and they keep charging my card ( {$35.00} annually every year totalling {$140.00} ). I have disputed with my Citi credit card multiple times and they fabricate documentation ti justify charges as if Im receiving the notices and that I need to reach to them to cancel. 

As I explained to Citi and XXXX, I did not have access to my email address for my university account since I XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  ). Thus, I could not have had access to the notification for the subscription renewal only after seeing the charge on my credit card. Everytime this happened I tried disputing charges ( a clear signal I did not want this ) and they kept charging me. This current year was the final straw. 

During last years dispute, I even went ahead and told Citi to just cancel my card number, give me a new one to see the final charge from XXXX. To my amazement this year, the charge same back again. How is it possible they could continue charging my Citi card without an active credit card on file? The card if not mistaken was even expired including my drivers license.Hence, XXXX didnt even have valid informaiton of me to charge nor provide me this service subscription which they gladly kept charging for throughout the years. 

Then during the dispute, its seems like XXXX autogenerated documentation to deceptively create the impression I had received notice of the charge which is not true. The documentation clearly shows a card which is no longer valid this year yet the charge went through successfully. I requested proof I had received the email or accepted the renewal and they just closed my ticket, said they refunded the card ( which they dont have latest on file! ), nor did I hear back on my support ticket ( never receive notice the support ticket was closed ) nor did i get any money back! 

I consider XXXX practices to be clear UDAP violations ( Unfair, Deceptive, or Abusive Acts or Practices ).
